question: Based on Tables 5.1-1 and 5.1-2, is the RCS volume adequate for the thermal-hydraulic operating conditions?

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control systems, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary system boundaries, and references where further design information can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is an integral part of the reactor vessel, designed to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is sized to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance by establishing steady-state parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressure levels—across different power levels using thermal hydraulic modeling and verification. The evaluation considers uncertainties and design margins to ensure efficient operation, maximize electrical generation, and maintain safety, particularly emphasizing the natural circulation design and low pressure losses in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) Code cases, as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a and related NRC regulatory guides. It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet regulatory conditions, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the methodology used to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement due to neutron exposure, and provides the relevant figures and tables that define acceptable operating conditions to prevent non-ductile failure of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures for maintaining the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel. It details the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation over its 60-year design life, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, all surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and provides structural support and attachment points for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the primary degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's novel helical tube and support design does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks assessed as low.
